tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P1] entities/session/lib/context-health.ts — the ONE percent/threshold/severity module + unit tests
tasks.description: Create apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/lib/context-health.ts — a pure, dependency-light module in the entities/session layer (the lowest FSD layer every consumer reaches: features/status, features/chat, features/session-list, and entities/session itself all sit at or above entities). TSDoc on every export (Hard Rule 4). Export EXACTLY: (a) const CONTEXT_WARNING_PERCENT = 80 — context usage at/above which a session is 'near full' (amber); (b) const CONTEXT_CRITICAL_PERCENT = 95 — usage at/above which a session is 'at the ceiling' (red); (c) type ContextSeverity = 'ok' | 'warning' | 'critical'; (d) function contextSeverity(percent: number): ContextSeverity — returns 'critical' when percent >= CONTEXT_CRITICAL_PERCENT, else 'warning' when percent >= CONTEXT_WARNING_PERCENT, else 'ok'; (e) function deriveContextPercent(tokens: number | null | undefined, maxTokens: number | null | undefined): number | null — the SINGLE formula min(100, round(tokens / maxTokens * 100)); returns null when either input is missing/non-positive (guards divide-by-zero and non-positive windows); (f) function resolveDisplayContextPercent(estimatePercent: number | null, contextUsage?: ContextUsage | null): number | null — returns the SDK breakdown's exact contextUsage.percentage when a rich ContextUsage is present (mirrors ContextItem's current 'prefer contextUsage.percentage, else the passed estimate' resolution), otherwise the coarser estimatePercent. Import the ContextUsage type from '@dorkos/shared' (the ContextUsageSchema-inferred type at packages/shared/src/schemas.ts:779). Export all five public symbols from the entities/session barrel apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts (add a module-level @module note if a new lib grouping is introduced). Write apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/lib/__tests__/context-health.test.ts (each case a purpose comment, no always-pass): deriveContextPercent computes the formula (e.g. 90000/180000 -> 50), returns null on null/undefined tokens, on null/undefined/zero/negative maxTokens, and caps at 100 when tokens exceed the window; contextSeverity boundaries are exact — 79 -> 'ok', 80 -> 'warning', 94 -> 'warning', 95 -> 'critical'; resolveDisplayContextPercent prefers a present contextUsage.percentage over the estimate and falls back to the estimate when contextUsage is null/undefined. This module lands FIRST so no later phase (the two new fleet surfaces) can add a fifth percent copy. Files: apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/lib/context-health.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/lib/__tests__/context-health.test.ts. Acceptance: the module compiles standalone; all unit tests pass; the five symbols are exported from the entities/session barrel. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/lib/__tests__/context-health.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (the ONE shared percent source — exact signatures), §Implementation Phases (Phase 1). No dependencies; runs fully in parallel with all of Phase 2 (client vs shared/server/test-utils, zero file overlap).
tasks.activeForm: Building the shared context-health module and its unit tests

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P1] Migrate the four percent sites + the compaction-chip threshold onto context-health (de-dup, no behavior change)
tasks.description: Consolidate the four existing percent-derivation copies and the duplicated chip threshold onto the shared module from task 1.1, with NO behavior change — every migrated site's EXISTING tests must keep passing against the shared source (that is the regression proof). Migrate all five sites: (1) apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/ContextItem.tsx:22,24 — replace the inline 'prefer contextUsage.percentage else the passed estimate' resolution (line 22) with resolveDisplayContextPercent, and replace the inline amber/red magic numbers (line 24, no named constant today) with contextSeverity, mapping the band to the existing classes: 'critical' -> 'text-red-500', 'warning' -> 'text-amber-500', 'ok' -> ''. (2) apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/status/use-compaction-chip.ts:23 — DELETE the local COMPACTION_CHIP_THRESHOLD_PERCENT = 80 constant AND its 'if the amber threshold ever moves, move this one too' caveat comment; import CONTEXT_WARNING_PERCENT and use it. (3) apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/stream/derive-status-bar.ts:39-43 — replace the local deriveContextPercent (totalTokens / maxTokens) with the shared deriveContextPercent. (4) apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-status.ts:92-94 — replace the inline percent = min(100, round(tokens / window * 100)) with the shared deriveContextPercent. (5) apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx:264-269 — replace the inline duplicate of ContextItem's resolution (commented 'Mirrors ContextItem\'s own percent resolution') with resolveDisplayContextPercent. IMPORT DISCIPLINE (FSD, Hard Rule 1): the four cross-slice/cross-layer sites (ContextItem in features/status; use-compaction-chip, derive-status-bar, ChatStatusSection in features/chat) import from the entities/session barrel ('@/layers/entities/session'); the SAME-slice site use-session-status.ts (entities/session/model) imports the sibling module directly via the relative path '../lib/context-health' to avoid a self-referential barrel import. After migration there is EXACTLY ONE formula and ONE threshold set across every context surface; the old inline thresholds and duplicate formulas are gone. Run each touched site's existing test file and confirm it still passes unchanged (they now share the constant); if a site has no existing test asserting its percent/threshold, add a short regression assertion (purpose comment) that it colors/thresholds identically post-migration. Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/status/ui/ContextItem.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/status/use-compaction-chip.ts, apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/stream/derive-status-bar.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-status.ts, apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx, plus the existing test files for each. Acceptance: all five sites import from context-health; no inline percent formula or threshold magic number remains at these sites; every pre-existing test at the migrated sites still passes (no behavior change); client typecheck + lint pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/status apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/status apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/stream apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (Migrate the four existing sites onto it), §Background (the four copies + the chip dup), Acceptance Criteria (exactly one client percent-derivation + threshold source). Depends on 1.1 (the shared module). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 2 (client-only; zero overlap with shared/server/test-utils).
tasks.activeForm: Migrating the four percent sites and the chip threshold onto context-health

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P2] SessionSchema: new optional lastAutoCompactAt + documented contextTokens + OpenAPI regen (additive, non-viral)
tasks.description: Extend SessionSchema in packages/shared/src/schemas.ts:112-127 with two minimal, OPTIONAL changes — both are ADDITIVE and NON-VIRAL: because both fields are optional, no existing consumer breaks and the workspace stays green after THIS task alone, so (unlike DOR-109's required-field change) NO atomic compile-gate bundling is needed here. (1) Add TSDoc to the existing contextTokens: z.number().int().optional() (line 124, field UNCHANGED) documenting the broadened semantics: a best-effort context-window token count (input + cache-read + cache-creation, per sumContextTokens) now carried on the LIST wire by claude-code from its JSONL tail (mtime-cached) as well as on a single-session read; ABSENT when no reading is available (codex/opencode closed-session rows, or an unreadable tail) so the client shows an honest 'unknown' gauge, never a fabricated 0%; percent is derived client-side against the model's context window (ModelOption.contextWindow). (2) Add a NEW field lastAutoCompactAt: z.string().datetime().optional() with TSDoc: an ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent AUTO-triggered context compaction visible in the session's readable transcript tail (claude-code only; codex has no compaction, opencode reports it live-only); ABSENT means no auto-compaction is visible in the tail (the session never auto-compacted, or the boundary scrolled past the ~16 KB tail window as the session grew — an honest, disclosed limitation; durable recency is a deferred follow-up); drives the row's discreet 'auto-compacted' marker. Keep both fields FLAT (no nested contextHealth object — the flat style matches the sibling optional fields); add NO maxTokens field on the DTO (the window stays a client catalog join via ModelOption.contextWindow) and NO RuntimeCapabilities change, NO conf migration, NO SQLite change (Decision 5). Regenerate the OpenAPI document so the schema change lands and the openapi-fresh CI check (.github/workflows/docs-openapi-check.yml) stays green: run pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build, then pnpm docs:export-api (regenerates docs/api/openapi.json from the Zod schemas via scripts/export-openapi.ts). Do NOT hand-edit openapi.json. Files: packages/shared/src/schemas.ts, docs/api/openapi.json (regenerated). Acceptance: shared build + typecheck pass; SessionSchema carries both fields (contextTokens documented, lastAutoCompactAt new optional datetime); docs/api/openapi.json is regenerated and openapi-fresh is green; no downstream consumer requires a change (both fields optional -> workspace green). Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck && pnpm docs:export-api. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 2 (DTO delta — verbatim schema), §API / data model changes, Acceptance Criteria (no RuntimeCapabilities change, no conf migration, no SQLite change). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 1; gates 2.2 and 2.3 (both need the field types).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the optional DTO fields and regenerating OpenAPI

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P2] claude-code transcript-reader: extend readTailStatus (lastAutoCompactAt) + fold tail into extractSessionMeta (mtime-cached) + simplify getSession + tests
tasks.description: In apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/transcript-reader.ts, wire the existing tail read into the mtime-cached, list-producing path so a closed-tab claude-code session carries a best-effort reading. The @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk boundary is untouched — this uses fs, the existing sumContextTokens (imported from ../sdk/context-tokens.js at line 14), and the existing compact_boundary JSONL shape only (Hard Rule 2 respected; no new SDK surface). THREE coordinated changes: (a) EXTEND readTailStatus (private, ~lines 164-224) to also return lastAutoCompactAt. In the SAME forward line-scan it already runs over the 16 KB tail (TRANSCRIPT.TAIL_BUFFER_BYTES = 16384), capture the top-level timestamp of the most recent record where type === 'system' && subtype === 'compact_boundary' && compactMetadata?.trigger === 'auto' (IGNORE trigger:'manual' — user-driven, not a pressure signal). New return shape: { model?, permissionMode?, contextTokens?, lastAutoCompactAt? }. (b) FOLD the tail read into extractSessionMeta (~lines 230-348) so the cached Session carries contextTokens and lastAutoCompactAt (and the tail's latest model overlays the head's model, matching what getSession does today). The enriched Session is stored in metaCache under its mtimeMs (the set at line 125), so listSessionsInDir (line 103) emits rows with a reading for every CHANGED session and serves settled sessions from cache with no re-read. Honor the cache contract exactly: cache HIT (mtimeMs unchanged) -> fs.stat only, no head/tail read; cache MISS (mtimeMs changed/absent) -> one 8 KB head read (HEAD_BUFFER_BYTES) + one 16 KB tail read, then cache the enriched Session. Net added cost is one 16 KB read per CHANGED claude-code session per list call — O(changed files), not O(all files). (c) SIMPLIFY getSession (~lines 145-153) to return extractSessionMeta directly — its separate readTailStatus overlay (lines 150-153) is now redundant; there must be EXACTLY ONE tail-read code path feeding both list and single-session reads, both enriched identically (no half-migration, no dead overlay left behind). invalidate(sessionId) (line 39) still drops the cache entry after a rename — unchanged. codex/opencode listSessions are NOT touched (they have no tail concept; their closed rows stay token-less -> unknown). Add/extend transcript-reader tests (apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/__tests__/, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): a listed session whose tail has usage carries contextTokens; a tail with a compact_boundary + trigger:'auto' yields lastAutoCompactAt; a trigger:'manual'-only tail yields NO lastAutoCompactAt; the mtime cache serves a SECOND list call with NO re-read (assert via a read/fs spy) and RE-reads after an mtime bump; a session with no tail tokens OMITS contextTokens (-> unknown downstream). Files: apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/transcript-reader.ts and its __tests__ file(s). Acceptance: server typecheck + lint pass; listed claude-code sessions carry contextTokens + lastAutoCompactAt from the tail; getSession has a single tail-read path (the redundant overlay is gone); the cache contract holds (no re-read on unchanged mtime, re-read after a bump); all tests pass. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/sessions/__tests__. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (the tail-read fold + the cache-contract table), §Testing Strategy (Server — tail read + cache), Acceptance Criteria (list carries contextTokens/lastAutoCompactAt, mtime-cached). Depends on 2.1 (SessionSchema must carry lastAutoCompactAt for the Session object to compile). Runs in parallel with Phase 1 (client) and 2.3 (test-utils/aggregation).
tasks.activeForm: Folding the tail read into the claude-code list path and simplifying getSession

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P2] FakeAgentRuntime + list fixtures carry the reading; aggregation/degradation tests (ADR-0310)
tasks.description: Give the test double and shared fixtures the ability to EXERCISE a list reading, and prove the ADR-0310 aggregation degrades honestly per runtime — without forcing any runtime to produce a reading. (a) In packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts, ensure listSessions (line 128, a vi.fn returning Session[]) and any list-producing fixtures/mock-factories (packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts) can emit Session rows that carry contextTokens and lastAutoCompactAt where a test exercises a reading — add/extend a fixture helper so a claude-code-shaped fake row CAN carry a reading and a codex/opencode-shaped row OMITS it. Do NOT make the fields mandatory anywhere — a token-less row must stay valid (that IS the codex/opencode reality). (b) Aggregation/degradation tests against apps/server/src/services/session/aggregate-session-list.ts (using FakeAgentRuntime + scenarios from @dorkos/test-utils; each a purpose comment, no always-pass), in apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/aggregate-session-list.test.ts (extend the existing aggregation test, or create it if absent): with a claude-code runtime producing rows that carry contextTokens/lastAutoCompactAt and codex/opencode runtimes producing token-less rows, the merged SessionListResponse carries readings ONLY for the claude-code rows while the others' rows OMIT contextTokens (-> unknown downstream); a REJECTING runtime contributes a warnings[] entry and zero rows (ADR-0310 per-runtime degradation) and NEVER fails the aggregate. (c) Confirm via runtimeConformance (packages/test-utils/src/runtime-conformance.ts) that NO adapter is forced to produce a reading — the conformance suite must not assert contextTokens presence for any runtime (add a purpose-commented non-forcing assertion only if warranted; do not weaken existing conformance). Files: packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts, packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts, apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/aggregate-session-list.test.ts, and packages/test-utils/src/runtime-conformance.ts (only if a non-forcing assertion is added). Acceptance: test-utils + server typecheck pass; the aggregation test proves per-runtime reading presence/absence and warnings[] degradation; runtimeConformance passes for every runtime and forces no reading. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/test-utils typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/session/__tests__/aggregate-session-list.test.ts. Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (Server — aggregation/degradation), §Background (the honest per-runtime reality), Acceptance Criteria (codex/opencode omit; warnings[] degrades; runtimeConformance passes), ADR-0310. Depends on 2.1 (the optional DTO fields the fixtures set). Runs in parallel with Phase 1 (client) and 2.2 (transcript-reader).
tasks.activeForm: Adding fixtures and the aggregation/degradation tests

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P3] session-list-store: retained contextReadings map + useSessionContextReading selector + store retention tests
tasks.description: In apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/session-list-store.ts, add a retained per-session LIVE context reading that survives settle, WITHOUT changing the existing liveness/border prune (Decision 3 — split the two concerns into two maps so the liveness prune can never cross-regress the retained reading). Add: (a) an interface SessionContextReading { contextUsage: SessionContextUsage; receivedAt: string } (TSDoc: a retained per-session live context reading; contextUsage carries totalTokens + maxTokens; receivedAt is the client receive time ISO, the reading's freshness stamp). Import SessionContextUsage from the shared session-stream type (already used in this slice via session-stream-store.ts:32). (b) a new store field contextReadings: Record<string, SessionContextReading>, initialized {}. In applyListEvent's session_status case (~lines 137-166): SET contextReadings[event.sessionId] = { contextUsage: event.status.contextUsage, receivedAt: new Date().toISOString() } WHENEVER event.status.contextUsage is present; and CRUCIALLY do NOT prune contextReadings on the idle/interrupted branch (lines 160-163) — the existing statuses/statusCwds prune STAYS EXACTLY AS TODAY (the liveness/border signal remains memory-bounded: settled -> no entry -> no border, unchanged), but the reading is RETAINED. Clear contextReadings[id] in the SAME places statuses is cleared: session_removed (lines 131-134), the retiredSessionId rekey-retire branch (lines 147-153), and resetStatuses (line 194, on stream reconnect — so a stale reading can't survive a server restart the way statuses already can't). (c) a selector export useSessionContextReading(sessionId: string): SessionContextReading | null (TSDoc), exported from the entities/session barrel (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts). Add/extend store tests (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/session-list-store.test.ts, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): a session_status with contextUsage populates contextReadings; a FOLLOWING idle/interrupted event prunes statuses[id] but RETAINS contextReadings[id]; session_removed, rekey-retire (retiredSessionId), and resetStatuses each CLEAR contextReadings[id]. Files: apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/session-list-store.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts, the store's __tests__ file. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; contextReadings retained across settle, pruned on remove/rekey/reset; the existing statuses/statusCwds prune is unchanged. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/session-list-store.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 5 (retain the settled reading — the two-map split), §Testing Strategy (Client — store retention), Acceptance Criteria (a settled session retains its last reading). INDEPENDENCE NOTE: this task needs ONLY the existing SessionContextUsage type — it has no hard dependency on the shared module (1.1) or the DTO (2.1), so it may begin as soon as Phase 3 opens and can run concurrently with Phases 1 and 2. It is the data-layer foundation the merge hook (3.2) builds on.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the retained contextReadings map and its selector

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P3] useSessionContextHealth — the list-vs-live merge resolver (live wins) + hook tests
tasks.description: Create the per-session merge resolver in entities/session (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-context-health.ts) that produces a row's context reading with LIVE-WINS precedence. Signature: useSessionContextHealth(session: Session): { status: 'known' | 'unknown'; percent?: number; severity?: ContextSeverity; autoCompactedAt?: string; fresh: boolean; asOf: string } (TSDoc). Precedence (spec §6): (1) LIVE — if useSessionContextReading(session.id) (the 3.1 selector) is present -> percent = deriveContextPercent(reading.contextUsage.totalTokens, reading.contextUsage.maxTokens); status 'known'; fresh: true; asOf = reading.receivedAt. (2) LIST — else if session.contextTokens is present -> percent = deriveContextPercent(session.contextTokens, window), where window = ModelOption.contextWindow for session.model resolved from useModels({ runtime: session.runtime }) (cached, deduped by query key across rows); status 'known'; fresh: false; asOf = session.updatedAt. (3) UNKNOWN — neither a live reading nor session.contextTokens, OR no window resolvable from the catalog -> { status: 'unknown', fresh: false, asOf: session.updatedAt }; NEVER a fabricated 0%. When status is 'known', severity = contextSeverity(percent). autoCompactedAt = session.lastAutoCompactAt, carried through in ALL branches when present (so the discreet marker can render even on an unknown-percent row). Import deriveContextPercent/contextSeverity/ContextSeverity from context-health (task 1.1), useSessionContextReading from the store selector (task 3.1), useModels from entities/session/model/use-models.ts, and the Session type from '@dorkos/shared'. Same-slice imports use sibling relative paths (../lib/context-health, ../model/...) NOT the self-barrel; cross-package types from '@dorkos/shared'. Export useSessionContextHealth from the entities/session barrel. Tests (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/use-session-context-health.test.tsx — RTL renderHook with TransportProvider + mocked useModels + a seeded store, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): LIVE wins over LIST (a live reading overrides session.contextTokens); LIST-only resolves via the model catalog window and is marked fresh:false with asOf === session.updatedAt; no live + no contextTokens -> status 'unknown'; a model ABSENT from the catalog -> 'unknown' (not 0%); autoCompactedAt passes through from session.lastAutoCompactAt. Files: apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-session-context-health.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts, the hook's __tests__ file. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; live-wins precedence and honest unknown are proven. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/use-session-context-health.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 6 (the merge rule — returned shape verbatim), §Testing Strategy (Client — merge rule), Acceptance Criteria (live wins; catalog-derived percent; a model with no window -> unknown, not 0%). This is the CONFLUENCE where Phase 1 (context-health) and Phase 2 (DTO fields) join the Phase 3 data layer. Depends on 1.1 (context-health), 2.1 (session.contextTokens/lastAutoCompactAt), and 3.1 (useSessionContextReading).
tasks.activeForm: Building the list-vs-live merge resolver hook

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P3] useFleetContextRollup — runtime-neutral fleet counts (near-full/critical/unknown/auto-compacted) + tests
tasks.description: Create the fleet rollup selector in entities/session (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-fleet-context-rollup.ts), shaped after use-agent-hottest-status.ts's fold. useFleetContextRollup(): FleetContextRollup where interface FleetContextRollup { total: number; known: number; unknown: number; warning: number; critical: number; autoCompacted: number } (TSDoc on the interface and each field: total = sessions considered; known = sessions with a resolvable reading; unknown = no reading (fabricate nothing); warning = severity 'warning' (>=80, <95); critical = severity 'critical' (>=95); autoCompacted = sessions with lastAutoCompactAt present). Compose useSessionListSessions() (session-list-store.ts:209) and resolve EACH session via the §6 rule from task 3.2 — REUSE useSessionContextHealth's resolution logic; do NOT reimplement the resolution or it becomes a fifth/sixth percent copy. If the per-row hook cannot be called inside a fold (rules-of-hooks), EXTRACT the pure resolution core from 3.2 into a shared helper (e.g. resolveSessionContextHealth(session, { reading, window })) that BOTH the per-row hook and this rollup call — keep exactly one resolution rule. 'Near full' in the summary copy = warning + critical. Build it runtime-neutral and surface-agnostic (the staged agents-dashboard tile, Decision 1, reuses the SAME rollup later, so no surface-specific shaping here). Export useFleetContextRollup (and, if extracted, the shared FleetContextRollup type) from the entities/session barrel. Tests (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/use-fleet-context-rollup.test.tsx — RTL with a seeded MIXED fleet + mocked useModels, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): counts across a mixed fleet (known/warning/critical/unknown/auto-compacted); 'near full' equals warning + critical; an UNKNOWN row is counted in unknown and NEVER as a reading (not folded into warning/critical or into a percent); autoCompacted counts sessions with lastAutoCompactAt regardless of percent state. Files: apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/use-fleet-context-rollup.ts, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts, the rollup's __tests__ file, and (if extracted) the shared resolution helper alongside 3.2's hook. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; counts correct; unknown never counted as a reading; a single resolution rule is shared with 3.2. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/model/__tests__/use-fleet-context-rollup.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 7 (the fleet rollup selector — interface verbatim), §Testing Strategy (Client — rollup), Acceptance Criteria (counts come from the rollup and exclude unknown rows). Depends on 3.2 (the §6 resolution rule it reuses; transitively on 1.1/2.1/3.1).
tasks.activeForm: Building the fleet context rollup selector

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P4] SessionContextGauge on SessionRow — quiet severity gauge + auto-compacted marker + honest unknown + a11y + RTL
tasks.description: Add a small SessionContextGauge component (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/SessionContextGauge.tsx) fed by useSessionContextHealth(session) (task 3.2), and place it on the row. Design-system 'quiet, not alarm' (designing-frontend skill; Calm Tech). STATES: KNOWN — a compact percent with a severity-tinted treatment read off the returned severity ('ok' -> muted/no tint, 'warning' -> amber, 'critical' -> red, matching ContextItem's vocabulary via the shared contextSeverity); PREFER a small ring/bar over a loud badge — glanceable, not shouting. AUTO-COMPACTED MARKER — when health.autoCompactedAt is present, a discreet glyph (e.g. a small Layers/recycle mark) with tooltip 'Auto-compacted {relative time} to free up context.' (presence-in-tail is the recency signal; the tooltip shows the time so the operator judges recency). UNKNOWN — a MUTED glyph, no number, no color, tooltip 'Context usage isn\'t available for this session yet. Open it to see live usage.' — NEVER a 0%. LIST-READING STALENESS — when fresh === false and status is 'known', the tooltip carries the extra line 'as of {relative updatedAt}.' All microcopy VERBATIM from spec §User Experience (writing-for-humans). a11y: the gauge root carries aria-label 'Context {N}% full' (known) or 'Context usage unknown'; decorative ring/dot elements are aria-hidden; the gauge must NOT steal the row's click target (it is presentational within the role="button" row). PLACEMENT: line-1 icon cluster in apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/SessionRowFull.tsx:151 (beside the pending/bypass icons). The row today receives only session: Session and derives its border via useSessionBorderState(session.id); the gauge takes session and calls useSessionContextHealth itself. Export SessionContextGauge from the entities/session barrel. Tests (apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/__tests__/SessionContextGauge.test.tsx — RTL, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): renders KNOWN with the correct severity treatment (amber >=80, red >=95); renders the MUTED unknown glyph + the unknown tooltip copy (no number); renders the auto-compacted marker + its tooltip when autoCompactedAt present; a11y label present for known and unknown; the gauge does NOT capture the row's click. Files: apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/SessionContextGauge.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/SessionRowFull.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/index.ts, the gauge's __tests__ file. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; each row shows the quiet gauge with the three honest states + a11y labels. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/entities/session/ui/__tests__/SessionContextGauge.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 8a (per-row gauge), §9 (honesty / unknown states), §User Experience (verbatim copy), Acceptance Criteria (each row shows a quiet context gauge). Depends on 3.2 (useSessionContextHealth). Runs in parallel with 4.2 (different files: entities/session/ui gauge + row vs features/session-list/ui bar + view).

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P4] FleetContextBar on the Sessions/Overview tab — multi-count summary + all-healthy/hidden states + a11y + RTL
tasks.description: Add a FleetContextBar (apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/FleetContextBar.tsx) modeled on RelayHealthBar (apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/RelayHealthBar.tsx): REUSE the border-b px-3 py-1.5 text-xs bar shell and a DOT_COLORS-style severity->dot map, but build a NEW multi-count row (RelayHealthBar is single-message). Fed by useFleetContextRollup() (task 3.3). COPY (verbatim, spec §User Experience, writing-for-humans): ALL HEALTHY -> 'All sessions have room.'; WITH PRESSURE -> '{n} near full · {m} auto-compacted' where n = warning + critical and m = autoCompacted, DROPPING a clause when its count is 0; HIDDEN ENTIRELY when the fleet has ZERO known readings AND ZERO near-full/compacted sessions (nothing to say -> no bar — NEVER render '0 near full'). Use the same dot-color + aria-hidden-dot patterns; if a count links to a filtered view, use a real <button>. PLACEMENT DECISION (DECOMPOSE picks one; the component stays placement-agnostic): place it at the TOP of SessionsView's scroll region, ABOVE the list, mirroring the existing warnings block precedent at apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionsView.tsx:64-70 — chosen over the SessionSidebar cross-tab placement (SessionSidebar.tsx:85-87) because it sits with the same-surface warnings[] block it complements and keeps the bar scoped to the sessions list; a later move is cheap. The whole-runtime-failure case is ALREADY surfaced by the ADR-0310 warnings[] block in SessionsView, so the bar does NOT re-report it. Tests (apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/__tests__/FleetContextBar.test.tsx — RTL with a mocked useFleetContextRollup, each a purpose comment, no always-pass): renders the multi-count row for a fleet with pressure ('N near full · M auto-compacted'); renders 'All sessions have room.' when known > 0 and no pressure; is HIDDEN (renders nothing) when zero known readings and zero near-full/compacted; DROPS the zero clause when one count is 0; a11y dots are aria-hidden. Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/FleetContextBar.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionsView.tsx, the features/session-list barrel (if it re-exports UI), the bar's __tests__ file. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; the bar shows honest counts, the all-healthy line, drops zero clauses, and hides when there is nothing to say. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/__tests__/FleetContextBar.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 8b (fleet summary bar), §User Experience (verbatim copy), Acceptance Criteria (fleet summary bar, hidden when nothing to report). Depends on 3.3 (useFleetContextRollup). Runs in parallel with 4.1.

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P4] E2E (optional): gauge on rows + summary bar + honest unknown row in the cockpit
tasks.description: Add a Playwright e2e (apps/e2e) verifying the fleet surfaces in the live cockpit backed by the test-mode runtime, following the browser-testing skill + existing apps/e2e patterns. Assert: the session list renders a context gauge on rows; the Sessions/Overview tab renders the fleet summary bar; an 'unknown' row (a runtime/closed session with no reading) reads as a MUTED, deliberate state (aria-label 'Context usage unknown' or the unknown tooltip copy) — NOT a broken or 0% state. Keep it RESILIENT to test-mode's data shape (assert presence + the honest unknown state, not exact percentages that depend on fixtures). This task is OPTIONAL-but-recommended per the spec (§Testing Strategy — E2E); it is priority medium and may be deferred without blocking the feature's RTL-verified acceptance. Files: apps/e2e/<fleet-context-health spec>. Acceptance: the e2e passes headless; a gauge and the summary bar are present; the unknown row reads muted, not broken. Verify: the apps/e2e Playwright run for the new spec (the repo browsertest entry point / pnpm --filter @dorkos/e2e test <spec>). Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (E2E, optional). Depends on 4.1 (gauge) and 4.2 (bar). Priority medium (optional).

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P4] User-facing prose: docs note (per-row + fleet context health, honest per-runtime) + changelog fragment (Added)
tasks.description:
Write the two user-facing prose deliverables, both in the writing-for-humans voice (plain enough for a smart 9th grader who doesn't code; benefit-first; honesty gate). (a) DOCS NOTE: add a short user-facing note on the docs/ page where session-list features are described (search docs/ for the sessions/cockpit/session-list page; grep for 'session list', 'Sessions', 'Overview') explaining that context health now appears per-row (a small gauge showing how full each session's context is) and as a fleet summary ('N near full · N auto-compacted'), and — framed HONESTLY — that some runtimes show 'unknown' until a session is open (do NOT claim codex/opencode fleet readings that don't exist; describe what the user sees). If a NEW doc page is added, register it in contributing/INDEX.md and confirm pnpm docs:coverage stays green. (b) CHANGELOG FRAGMENT: create changelog/unreleased/<id>-fleet-context-health.md where <id> is a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts (filename <YYMMDD-HHMMSS>-fleet-context-health.md per changelog/README.md; NO frontmatter; Keep-a-Changelog headings). This is a headline differentiator (no other coding-agent cockpit shows fleet context health), so it is an '### Added' entry, user-facing and benefit-first. Convey plainly: see at a glance how full each agent's context is, right in the session list — a small gauge on every row plus a fleet summary saying how many sessions are near full or just auto-compacted, so you know which agent to jump into before it runs out of room; note the honest per-runtime coverage (Claude Code sessions show a reading even when closed; other runtimes show usage once you open the session). NEVER claim an unverified end-to-end capability (demo-claim gate). Example shape (rewrite in your own plain words, keep it honest):
### Added
- See how full each agent's context is at a glance. Every session row now shows a small context gauge, and the sessions view sums up how many are near full or recently auto-compacted — so you jump into the right agent before it runs out of room. Claude Code sessions show a reading even when closed; other runtimes show it once you open the session. (DOR-113)
Files: docs/<sessions page>.mdx (or the existing session-list page), contributing/INDEX.md (only if a page is added), changelog/unreleased/<YYMMDD-HHMMSS>-fleet-context-health.md. Acceptance: the docs note reads honestly (no fabricated cross-runtime claim); the changelog fragment exists with a valid timestamp-id filename, an '### Added' heading, and passes the five writing-for-humans self-checks; docs:coverage green if a page was added. Verify: pnpm docs:coverage (if docs changed) + a manual writing-for-humans review (there is no automated prose test). Cites: spec §Documentation (docs note + changelog fragment), changelog/README.md, writing-for-humans skill. Depends on 4.1 and 4.2 (so the prose matches the shipped surfaces + copy). Priority medium.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the docs note and changelog fragment

tasks.subject: [fleet-context-health] [P4] Draft ADR (proposed) — fleet-level context health: best-effort list reading + live override, honest per-runtime degradation (extends ADR-0310)
tasks.description: Draft the proposed ADR the spec calls for (§Related ADRs). PREFER running /adr:from-spec against specs/fleet-context-health/02-specification.md (it applies the significance rubric at extraction and writes decisions/<id>-<slug>.md + the decisions/manifest.json entry using a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts); otherwise write it by hand following the writing-adrs skill (concise, decision-focused). STATUS: proposed. TITLE: 'Fleet-level context health — best-effort list reading + live override, honest per-runtime degradation'. CAPTURE: the two-layer data path (the claude-code JSONL tail-read list reading, mtime-cached, PLUS the shipped session_status live override where LIVE wins); the client-side percent derivation against the model catalog window (ModelOption.contextWindow) with a SINGLE shared source (entities/session/lib/context-health.ts) consolidating the previously-four copies; and the FIELD-ABSENCE + warnings[] honesty model that EXTENDS ADR-0310's per-runtime session-list degradation to a new DISPLAYED dimension (NO new RuntimeCapabilities flag — codex/opencode closed rows render 'unknown'). Note the deliberately DEFERRED follow-ups (durable lastCompactedAt persistence, the agents-dashboard tile, a codex registry patch, opencode sidecar message reads, a batched context-health endpoint). Cross-link: EXTENDS ADR-0310; the live override rides ADR-0263 / ADR-0264 (durable projection + single delivery); reference DOR-112 (CompactionChip — the threshold agreement hardened here by the shared constant) and DOR-100 (UsageStatus cost sibling — coordinated, not merged). Add the decisions/manifest.json entry with status 'proposed' and a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts. Files: decisions/<id>-fleet-context-health.md, decisions/manifest.json. Acceptance: decisions/<id>-<slug>.md exists with status proposed; context, decision, consequences, and the extends/related links are captured; decisions/manifest.json is valid JSON with the new proposed entry. Verify: the ADR renders; the id came from .claude/scripts/id.ts; decisions/manifest.json parses (node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('decisions/manifest.json','utf8'))"). Cites: spec §Related ADRs (Proposed ADR). Depends on 4.1 and 4.2 (the decision is fully realized once the surfaces ship). Priority low.
